title: NOC 52100 – Library and Public Archive Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Library and Public Archive Technicians under NOC 52100 are technical professionals who support librarians and archivists in managing collections, assisting users, and maintaining document systems across Canada. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation usually requires a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group52 – Technical occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group521 – Technical occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5210 – Technical occupations in libraries and public archivesNOC Code52100

duties: Assist users in accessing books, films, maps, documents, and electronic resources
Catalogue new library acquisitions under supervision
Conduct manual and online reference searches
Assist with library tours and specialized programs
Implement classification and records disposal plans
employment_requirements: Library technicians usually require completion of a two- to three-year college program in library and information technology
Public archive technicians require a college diploma or certificate in archive and document management technology
salary_prospects: Library and Public Archive Technicians typically earn:
$28,900 – $74,500 per year,depending on experience, province, and institution type.
job_outlook: Opportunities are strongest in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
